results.title: Limitation of Liability
results._source_text_exact: 7.1 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim.
results.original_text: 7.1 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim.
results.reasoning_evidence: 7.1 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed the value of the Purchase
results.quoted_text: Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim.
results.explanation_simple: The clause limits the Supplier's liability to the value of the Purchase Order.
results.why_it_matters: This limitation can significantly affect the financial exposure of the Supplier in case of a claim.
results.legal_insight: This clause affects how financial responsibility is allocated if losses, claims, or disputes arise.
results.market_comparison: This type of liability cap is common in commercial agreements, but the specific limit may vary.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause limits liability, which could leave Contracting Party A exposed in cases of significant loss.
results.recommendation: Check whether the liability cap, exclusions, and carve-outs are clearly stated and proportionate.
results.safer_alternative: Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed twice the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim, excluding cases of gross negligence.

section_path: 7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
7.1 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.

market_comparison.text_before_validation: This type of liability cap is common in commercial agreements, but the specific limit may vary.

safer_alternative.text_before_validation: Supplier's aggregate liability shall not exceed twice the value of the Purchase Order giving rise to the claim, excluding cases of gross negligence.

recommendation.text_before_validation: Check whether the liability cap, exclusions, and carve-outs are clearly stated and proportionate.

results.title: Time is of the Essence
results._source_text_exact: 1.2 Time is of the essence. If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty and procure substitute goods, with Supplier liable for any reasonable cover costs.
results.original_text: 1.2 Time is of the essence. If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty and procure substitute goods, with Supplier liable for any reasonable cover costs.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.2 Time is of the essence. If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel th
results.quoted_text: If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty and procure substitute goods, with Supplier liable for any reasonable cover costs.
results.explanation_simple: The clause establishes a strict delivery timeline and allows the Buyer to cancel the order if the Supplier fails to meet it.
results.why_it_matters: This clause creates a potential financial liability for the Supplier if they do not deliver on time, as they will be responsible for cover costs incurred by the Buyer.
results.legal_insight: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.
results.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in commercial agreements where timely delivery is critical.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause imposes a strict delivery requirement with potential financial liability for the Supplier, which could create significant exposure.
results.safer_alternative: Supplier shall deliver Goods within ten (10) business days of the scheduled delivery date, with Buyer having the option to cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty after that period.
section_path: 1. PURCHASE ORDERS AND DELIVERY

1.2 Time is of the essence. If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty and procure substitute goods, with Supplier liable for any reasonable cover costs.

legal_insight.text_before_validation: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.

market_comparison.text_before_validation: This clause is relatively standard in commercial agreements where timely delivery is critical.

safer_alternative.text_before_validation: Supplier shall deliver Goods within ten (10) business days of the scheduled delivery date, with Buyer having the option to cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty after that period.

results.title: Purchase Requirements
results._source_text_exact: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
results.original_text: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
results.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competit
results.quoted_text: Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
results.explanation_simple: The clause mandates that the Buyer must source a minimum of 70% of its Goods from the Supplier, contingent on the Supplier's competitiveness.
results.why_it_matters: This creates a significant obligation for the Buyer, potentially limiting their sourcing flexibility and exposing them to risks if the Supplier fails to meet competitive standards.
results.legal_insight: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause creates a purchasing obligation that could limit the Buyer's flexibility and expose them to risks if the Supplier fails to remain competitive.
section_path: 5. EXCLUSIVITY

5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.

results.title: Purchase Orders and Delivery
results._source_text_exact: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
results.original_text: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specificati
results.quoted_text: Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
results.explanation_simple: The clause outlines the timeline for submitting purchase orders and the supplier's obligation to deliver goods.
results.why_it_matters: Timely submission of purchase orders is crucial for ensuring that goods are delivered as needed, which can impact operational efficiency.
results.legal_insight: The requirement for the Buyer to submit purchase orders 60 days in advance establishes a clear timeline that helps manage expectations for delivery. This can reduce the risk of disputes over delivery timing.
results.recommendation: The clause's terms are reasonable as they provide a clear framework for order submission and delivery timelines.

results.title: Prices for Goods
results._source_text_exact: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
results.original_text: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
results.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no
results.quoted_text: Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
results.explanation_simple: The pricing structure allows for adjustments after the first year, which could impact costs.
results.why_it_matters: The cap on price increases provides some predictability, but adjustments based on raw material costs could lead to higher expenses for the buyer.
results.legal_insight: The clause allows for annual price adjustments after the initial fixed period, which could create financial exposure if raw material costs rise significantly. The 6% cap mitigates this risk to some extent, but it still introduces uncertainty in long-term budgeting.
results.market_comparison: This pricing structure is somewhat common, as fixed pricing with potential adjustments is often seen in supply agreements.
results.recommendation: The clause provides a reasonable balance between fixed pricing and the ability to adjust for material cost increases.

results.title: Inspection and Replacement of Goods
results._source_text_exact: 3.1 Buyer shall have the right to inspect Goods within fifteen (15) business days of delivery and reject any Goods that do not conform to the Specifications. Supplier shall replace non-conforming Goods at its own expense within ten (10) business days.

results.why_it_matters: This warranty provides assurance to the buyer regarding the quality and reliability of the Goods.
results.legal_insight: The warranty period of 24 months is a standard duration for product warranties, providing a reasonable timeframe for the buyer to identify defects.

results.title: Termination Rights
results._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.

results.quoted_text: Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.
results.explanation_simple: The clause allows termination for uncured material breaches with a notice period.
results.why_it_matters: It provides a clear process for termination, ensuring both parties have an opportunity to address breaches.
results.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
results.market_comparison: This termination clause is standard in commercial agreements, providing reasonable protection for both parties.

clauses.why_it_matters: This clause creates a potential financial liability for the Supplier if they do not deliver on time, as they will be responsible for cover costs incurred by the Buyer.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in commercial agreements where timely delivery is critical.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause imposes a strict delivery requirement with potential financial liability for the Supplier, which could create significant exposure.
clauses.safer_alternative: Supplier shall deliver Goods within ten (10) business days of the scheduled delivery date, with Buyer having the option to cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty after that period.
section_path: 1. PURCHASE ORDERS AND DELIVERY

1.2 Time is of the essence. If Supplier fails to deliver Goods within five (5) business days of the scheduled delivery date, Buyer may, at its option, cancel the affected Purchase Order without penalty and procure substitute goods, with Supplier liable for any reasonable cover costs.

section_path: 4. WARRANTY
4.1 Supplier warrants that the Goods will be free from defects in material and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from delivery, and will conform to the Specifications.

clauses.title: Purchase Requirements
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.

clauses.quoted_text: Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause mandates that the Buyer must source a minimum of 70% of its Goods from the Supplier, contingent on the Supplier's competitiveness.
clauses.why_it_matters: This creates a significant obligation for the Buyer, potentially limiting their sourcing flexibility and exposing them to risks if the Supplier fails to meet competitive standards.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause creates a purchasing obligation that could limit the Buyer's flexibility and expose them to risks if the Supplier fails to remain competitive.
section_path: 5. EXCLUSIVITY

clauses.title: Prices for Goods
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.original_text: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no
clauses.quoted_text: Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.explanation_simple: The pricing structure allows for adjustments after the first year, which could impact costs.
clauses.why_it_matters: The cap on price increases provides some predictability, but adjustments based on raw material costs could lead to higher expenses for the buyer.
clauses.legal_insight: The clause allows for annual price adjustments after the initial fixed period, which could create financial exposure if raw material costs rise significantly. The 6% cap mitigates this risk to some extent, but it still introduces uncertainty in long-term budgeting.
clauses.market_comparison: This pricing structure is somewhat common, as fixed pricing with potential adjustments is often seen in supply agreements.
clauses.recommendation: The clause provides a reasonable balance between fixed pricing and the ability to adjust for material cost increases.

section_path: 4. WARRANTY
4.1 Supplier warrants that the Goods will be free from defects in material and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from delivery, and will conform to the Specifications.

clauses.title: Purchase Requirements
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competit
clauses.quoted_text: Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause mandates that the Buyer must source a minimum of 70% of its Goods from the Supplier, contingent on the Supplier's competitiveness.
clauses.why_it_matters: This creates a significant obligation for the Buyer, potentially limiting their sourcing flexibility and exposing them to risks if the Supplier fails to meet competitive standards.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause may create legal or operational exposure.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause creates a purchasing obligation that could limit the Buyer's flexibility and expose them to risks if the Supplier fails to remain competitive.
section_path: 5. EXCLUSIVITY

5.1 During the Term, Buyer shall purchase no less than seventy percent (70%) of its requirements for the Goods from Supplier, provided Supplier remains competitive on price, quality, and delivery performance as benchmarked against the market.
risk_reasons: Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.

clauses.title: Purchase Orders and Delivery
clauses._source_text_exact: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
clauses.original_text: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specificati
clauses.quoted_text: Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause outlines the timeline for submitting purchase orders and the supplier's obligation to deliver goods.
clauses.why_it_matters: Timely submission of purchase orders is crucial for ensuring that goods are delivered as needed, which can impact operational efficiency.
clauses.legal_insight: The requirement for the Buyer to submit purchase orders 60 days in advance establishes a clear timeline that helps manage expectations for delivery. This can reduce the risk of disputes over delivery timing.
clauses.recommendation: The clause's terms are reasonable as they provide a clear framework for order submission and delivery timelines.

section_path: 1. PURCHASE ORDERS AND DELIVERY

1.1 Buyer shall submit Purchase Orders at least sixty (60) days prior to the requested delivery date. Supplier shall deliver Goods conforming to the Specifications on the dates set forth in each accepted Purchase Order.

clauses.title: Inspection and Replacement of Goods
clauses._source_text_exact: 3.1 Buyer shall have the right to inspect Goods within fifteen (15) business days of delivery and reject any Goods that do not conform to the Specifications. Supplier shall replace non-conforming Goods at its own expense within ten (10) business days.

clauses.quoted_text: Buyer shall have the right to inspect Goods within fifteen (15) business days of delivery and reject any Goods that do not conform to the Specifications. Supplier shall replace non-conforming Goods at its own expense within ten (10) business days.
clauses.explanation_simple: The Buyer can inspect Goods and reject those that do not meet specifications.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause ensures that the Buyer has the opportunity to verify the quality of the Goods received.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is standard in procurement agreements, allowing for inspection and replacement of non-conforming Goods.
clauses.recommendation: The clause's terms are reasonable as they provide a clear framework for inspection and replacement.

section_path: 3. QUALITY AND INSPECTION

3.1 Buyer shall have the right to inspect Goods within fifteen (15) business days of delivery and reject any Goods that do not conform to the Specifications. Supplier shall replace non-conforming Goods at its own expense within ten (10) business days.

clauses.title: Prices for Goods
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.original_text: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no
clauses.quoted_text: Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.
clauses.explanation_simple: The pricing structure allows for adjustments after the first year, which could impact costs.
clauses.why_it_matters: The cap on price increases provides some predictability, but adjustments based on raw material costs could lead to higher expenses for the buyer.
clauses.legal_insight: The clause allows for annual price adjustments after the initial fixed period, which could create financial exposure if raw material costs rise significantly. The 6% cap mitigates this risk to some extent, but it still introduces uncertainty in long-term budgeting.
clauses.market_comparison: This pricing structure is somewhat common, as fixed pricing with potential adjustments is often seen in supply agreements.
clauses.recommendation: The clause provides a reasonable balance between fixed pricing and the ability to adjust for material cost increases.

source_evidence.label: PRICING_ADJUSTMENT
source_evidence.text: Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year

section_path: 2. PRICING AND PAYMENT

2.1 Prices for the Goods are as set forth in Exhibit A and shall remain fixed for the first twelve (12) months of the Term, subject thereafter to adjustment no more than once annually based on documented increases in raw material costs, capped at 6% per year.

clauses.title: Termination Rights
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.
clauses.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.

clauses.quoted_text: Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause allows termination for uncured material breaches with a notice period.
clauses.why_it_matters: It provides a clear process for termination, ensuring both parties have an opportunity to address breaches.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
clauses.market_comparison: This termination clause is standard in commercial agreements, providing reasonable protection for both parties.

section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION
6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure.
